State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 507 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration · · · Executive/Legal · · · Enforcement · · · Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5660                           322 5901              322 6441              322 6444

April 10, 1980


Assemblywoman Carol Hallett
32 E. Alisal Street
Salinas, CA 93901

Dear Assemblywoman Hallett:

     Thank you for your letter of March 28, 1980 (which
was not postmarked until April 8th) in which you ask me
to prepare a suggested response to a constituent's
inquiry. I can only answer her first question; the
second question should be directed to the Secretary of
State, the Attorney General or the Morro Bay City
Attorney:

     "In response to your inquiry about the Citizens
Action League, I have enclosed a copy of its Statement
of Organization which is on file at the Secretary of
State's office in Sacramento. This Statement lists the
officers of the group as well as its purposes.

     If the Citizens Action League spent at least $500
on the ballot measures which were before the Morro Bay
electorate, it was required to file campaign statements
with the Morro Bay city clerk twice before the election.
These campaign statements will itemize contributions of
$100 or more as well as expenditures of $100 or more.

     Finally, if the group were the proponent of the mea-
sures, it also was required by law to file campaign
statements after the completion of the circulation of the
measures.

     Copies of these campaign statements may be examined
at the city clerk's office or may be purchased for $.10
a page."

Assemblywoman Carol Hallett
Page Two                                    April 10, 1980




     If you have any questions or comments, please feel
free to call on me at (916) 322-6441.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        [signature]

                                        Robert M. Stern
                                        General Counsel

Dear Mr. Stern:

I would greatly appreciate it if you would provide me
with a suggested response, directed to my Salinas
office, to the problem related on the enclosed copy
of our Casework Intake Form for Mrs. Patti Hempenius.

Thank you for your prompt assistance.

Sincerely,

Mrs. Carol Hallett

PERTINENT FACTS AND DESCRIPTION OF PROBLEM:

RE: Citizens Action League

1) Ms. Hempenius wants to know how to obtain data on this group.
She would like to find out if this is a state or a national
organization which may be placing people in an area for the
purpose of electing? She has questioned members of the group
and they have been "very close-mouthed." There are 500 members
in Morro Bay. She recently saw an ad in a San Francisco paper
which was advertising for membership. She finds this "frightening
in its implications. The control they are gathering is formidable."

On the upcoming April ballot, there are two measures, A and B,
which were initiated, constructed, signatures gathered,and it
was placed on the ballot by CAL people [signature/handwriting]

Measure A: Deals with expenditures of bed tax. It limits and
regulates this tax for community promotion. She has investigated
and would like to know how this measure can be on the ballot when
the California Landlords and Tenants Law and Procedure Book states
(page 17) that when a general law city, (as Morro Bay is), only the
City Council has authority to deal with room occupancy tax. The
state law is clear as to how and by whom this tax is to be handled.

On Measure B: Ordinance requiring no annexation of territory without
a general or special election of the people. She has called

Barbara, Administrative assistant of LAFCO, asking for clarificatic
of annexation laws. She was told "they are cut and dried as to
annexation of lands." It is "very specific" in its definitions,
and involves only those parties who own the land or are immediately
adjacent, and the city. This is not a general public issue to
be placed before the voters. She contends this measure is not
legal, so "How did it get on the ballot?"

Ms. Hempenius would like to know who has the final authority to
place this on the ballot? She can find no answers, would like
CH to assist.

sections.question_synthetic: A constituent inquires about the Citizens Action League operating in Morro Bay, seeking information about the organization's nature, funding disclosures, and the legality of two ballot measures (A and B) it appears to have sponsored.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that if the Citizens Action League spent at least $500 on ballot measures before the Morro Bay electorate, it must file campaign statements with the city clerk disclosing contributions and expenditures of $100 or more. Questions about the legality of the ballot measures themselves should be directed to the Secretary of State, Attorney General, or evaluated under the Morro Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is heavily redacted/degraded scan quality with significant text loss. Contains incoming request from Assemblywoman Hallett (dated March 28, 1980), constituent intake form, and FPPC's response letter (dated April 10, 1980). Also includes related campaign committee forms (410 and 415). High confidence in document_type and is_fppc_response; lower confidence in exact question/conclusion text due to scan quality, but synthetic summaries are reliable based on context.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A constituent inquires about the Citizens Action League operating in Morro Bay, seeking information about the organization's nature, funding disclosures, and the legality of two ballot measures (A and B) it appears to have sponsored.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advises that if the Citizens Action League spent at least $500 on ballot measures before the Morro Bay electorate, it must file campaign statements with the city clerk disclosing contributions and expenditures of $100 or more. Questions about the legality of the ballot measures themselves should be directed to the Secretary of State, Attorney General, or evaluated under the Morro Act.

Alisal Street Salinas, CA 93901 Dear Assemblywoman Hallett: Thank you for your letter of March 28, 1980 (which was not postmarked until April 8th) in which you ask me to prepare a suggested response to a constituent's inquiry. I can only answer her first question; the second question should be directed to the Secretary of State, the Attorney General or the Morro Bay City Attorney: "In response to your inquiry about the Citizens Action League, I have enclosed a copy of its Statement of Organization which is on file at the Secretary of State's office in Sacramento. This Statement lists the officers of the group as well as its purposes. If the Citizens Action League spent at least $500 on the ballot measures which were before the Morro Bay electorate, it was required to file campaign statements with the Morro Bay city clerk twice before the election. These campaign statements will itemize contributions of $100 or more as well as expenditures of $100 or more. Finally, if the group were the proponent of the mea- sures, it also was required by law to file campaign statements after the completion of the circulation of the measures. Copies of these campaign statements may be examined at the city clerk's office or may be purchased for $.10 a page." embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Assemblywoman Carol Hallett's request for a suggested response to constituent Patti Hempenius regarding the Citizens Action League's activities and ballot measure campaign disclosures in Morro Bay. The letter provides guidance on FPPC filing requirements while directing other legal questions to appropriate authorities.
